query: 北陸地方で泊まれる古民家を教えて
reasoning: RULE B applied: two shortfalls exist — (1) geographic scope is Ishikawa only, not all Hokuriku (Ishikawa+Toyama+Fukui+Niigata), and (2) the query explicitly requests 古民家 (kominka/old folk house stays) but the response returns onsen ryokan and chain hotels with no kominka filtering. The dominant failure is Retrieval (A): the tool args neither fan out to the full Hokuriku region nor filter by lodging_type for kominka. intent_understanding=2 because both intent (kominka) and scope (Hokuriku) are missed. constraint_handling=2 for same dual constraint failure. specificity=3 per RULE C: data returned is real/authoritative even if wrong category.
missing_data: 古民家 (kominka) lodging type filtering across all four Hokuriku prefectures (Ishikawa, Toyama, Fukui, Niigata). Should fan out get_hotels to all Hokuriku prefectures and surface entries with lodging_type or tags indicating machiya/kominka.
improvement_hint: Add kominka/machiya as a filterable lodging_type tag in the hotels dataset; support multi-prefecture fan-out in get_hotels (e.g. region='hokuriku') so agents can retrieve all four prefectures in one call. Alternatively, get_spots with kind=kominka_stay would route this query correctly.
